login.cardSubtitle: Use your work email and password.

errors.missingEmailForReset: Enter your email above first, then click reset.

login.footer: Account requests go through your administrator.

setup.title: Kanban setup
setup.subtitle: First-run configuration
setup.footer: Configuration is uploaded to your Flexweg site as config.js. Re-running the form overwrites it.

terms.title: Terms of use
terms.intro: Please read and accept the terms below before using Flexweg Kanban. They are written in plain language to be quick to read.

section1.title: 1. Free service
section1.body: Flexweg Kanban is provided to you free of charge by Flexweg. You can use it without limits with your Flexweg hosting — Flexweg will never bill you for using the Kanban itself. Standard charges for your Flexweg hosting plan and your Firebase plan still apply, on the respective accounts you own.

section2.title: 2. Provided as-is, no warranty
section2.body: The Kanban is provided as-is. We work to keep it stable and improve it over time, but we cannot guarantee it will be free of bugs, defects or interruptions. Use it with the same caution you would any tool you didn't write yourself: keep backups of important content.

section3.title: 3. Your content, your responsibility
section3.body: You are solely responsible for the content you publish through the Kanban — text, images, videos, forms, code embeds, and any other media. You must comply with applicable law in your jurisdiction (and in the jurisdictions you target), copyright, image and personality rights, personal data protection rules, and any third-party terms (e.g. embedded fonts or videos). Flexweg is not responsible for content published by users of the Kanban.

section4.title: 4. Your Firebase account, your security
section4.body: The Kanban stores its data in your own Firebase project. You are responsible for securing that project: choose a strong password for the bootstrap admin account, set up Firestore security rules as documented, rotate your API keys when needed, and review who has access. Flexweg cannot be held responsible for unauthorised access, data leaks, hacks or losses caused by misconfiguration or compromise of your Firebase account.

section5.title: 5. Limitation of liability
section5.body: Flexweg cannot be held responsible for any loss of revenue, loss of data, loss of business opportunity, or any direct or indirect damages arising from the use, misuse, or unavailability of the Kanban. Where applicable law requires Flexweg to assume some liability for the Kanban, that liability is limited to the amount actually paid to Flexweg for the Kanban over the past 12 months — which is zero, since the Kanban is free.

section6.title: 6. Your data stays yours
section6.body: Content you create with Flexweg Kanban is stored in your own Firebase project (tickets, sprints, comments, user records, workflow configuration) and on your own Flexweg hosting (ticket attachments uploaded via the Files API). Flexweg Kanban itself does not transmit your content to any third-party service.

section7.title: 7. Fair use
section7.body: Use of the Kanban for unlawful, fraudulent, or harmful activities, or in ways that breach Flexweg's general terms of use, may result in suspension of your Flexweg hosting access. We may also update these Kanban terms over time; significant changes will be communicated through the admin or via email.

terms.accept: I have read and accept the Terms of use.

welcome.heading: Welcome to your Kanban
welcome.intro: This first-run setup wires the Kanban to a Firebase project for data + authentication, then to your Flexweg site for ticket attachments. It takes about two minutes once you have a Firebase project.
welcome.firebaseBody: The Kanban needs a free Firebase project (Spark plan is fine — no billing required). You'll need its Web app config plus an Authentication user that will become the bootstrap admin. Email/Password sign-in must be enabled.

setup.intro: Enter your Firebase Web app config and the email of your bootstrap admin. The admin must already exist as a Firebase Auth user — create it manually in the Firebase Console first.
setup.introFlexweg: Provide your Flexweg API key — the Kanban uses Flexweg's Files API to upload ticket attachments AND to persist the config so every team member's browser boots without re-running this form.

help.firebase: Copy these values from Firebase Console → Project settings → Your apps → Web app config.
help.admin: The Firebase Auth user that gets admin rights without needing a Firestore users/{uid} record. Must already exist — create it from Firebase Console → Authentication → Users.
help.flexweg: Find these values in your Flexweg account dashboard.
help.flexwegApiKeyHint: Generate a permanent API key in your

errors.missingFields: Some required fields are missing or invalid.
errors.firebaseAuthInvalidCredentialHint: Double-check the admin email + password from the Firebase Console (Authentication → Users). Email/Password sign-in must be enabled for this project.
errors.wrongAdminEmail: The Firebase account you signed in with does not match the admin email you typed. Try again.
errors.firebaseAuthUserNotFound: No Firebase account matches that email.
errors.firebaseInvalidApiKey: Firebase API key is invalid. Re-check the apiKey field.
errors.firebaseTooManyRequests: Too many sign-in attempts. Wait a minute and try again.
errors.firebaseOperationNotAllowed: Email/Password sign-in is not enabled for this Firebase project. Enable it in Firebase Console → Authentication → Sign-in method.
errors.flexwegAuth: Flexweg API key rejected. Check the value from your Flexweg account → API.
errors.flexwegNetwork: Network error contacting Flexweg. Check the API base URL and your connection.
errors.firestoreRulesHint: Open Firebase Console → Firestore → Rules and confirm that the bootstrap admin email matches: {{email}}.
errors.uploadFailed: Could not upload config.js to Flexweg
